steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is the singular noun phrase "the Wheeling National Heritage Area." The sentence describes what that area currently is — a region that "has long been known" for its attributes. The verb must be singular and must match the present-tense context.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'was': past tense. The sentence uses "has long been known," indicating the area’s attributes extend into the present, so past tense is wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — 'is': singular present tense. Matches the singular subject and aligns with "has long been known." ✓
  • Choice C — 'have been': plural verb. Violates subject-verb agreement with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — 'will have been': future perfect tense. The sentence describes the area as it currently exists, not a future state. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
